name: Orion
description_short: Orion Financial is a member-owned federal credit union serving Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi with checking, savings, credit cards, auto loans, and home financing.
description_long:
Orion Financial, formerly known as Orion Federal Credit Union, is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee. The organization recently rebranded to reflect its expanded mission as a comprehensive financial partner for individuals, families, and businesses. As a federally-chartered credit union (NCUA-insured), Orion operates multiple branches across the Memphis area and serves members in Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi.

Orion Financial offers a diverse range of consumer and business financial products. Their consumer services include checking and savings accounts, credit cards with introductory 0% APR offers, auto loans starting at 3.45% APR with 90-day deferred payment options, home loans and HELOCs, and various educational resources on financial literacy. The credit union also provides business banking solutions, positioning itself as a partner for entrepreneurs and business owners alongside individual consumers. All deposit accounts are NCUA-insured, providing standard member protections.

Orion Financial differentiates itself through an emphasis on financial education and member belonging. The organization actively provides educational content on home buying, credit score improvement, savings strategies, and first-time credit card usage. They highlight a "lifelong partner" positioning rather than transactional banking, and their rebrand emphasizes commitment to guiding members through different life stages. The company also offers Spanish-language services at select locations (Germantown and Hacks Cross branches noted as "Se Habla Español").

As a credit union, Orion operates under different regulatory frameworks than traditional banks and prioritizes member value over shareholder profit. However, membership is restricted to residents of Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi, limiting accessibility. The organization's website content shows active updates and accessibility improvements, and they maintain regular member meetings, with their next annual meeting scheduled for March 18, 2026. Like most credit unions, Orion's product offerings and rates are competitive within the regional market but may have limitations compared to national financial institutions.
services: Auto loans (starting at 3.45% APR with 90-day deferred payments)
Business banking and business loan products
Member annual meetings and governance participation
pros: Auto loans with competitive 3.45% APR starting rate and 90-day deferred payment option
Credit card with 0% introductory APR for 6 months on purchases and balance transfers, zero annual fee
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members rather than shareholders
NCUA insurance protection on all member deposits (equivalent to FDIC for credit unions)
Comprehensive financial education resources covering home buying, credit scores, and savings
Multiple branch locations across Memphis area with extended Saturday hours and 24/7 ATM access
Spanish-language services available at select branch locations
cons: Membership restricted to residents of Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi—cannot serve customers outside service area
Credit card APR after introductory period (12.24%-17.99%) is higher than many national card issuers
Limited branch network compared to major national banks; primarily Memphis-area focused
Website indicates site is still under active accessibility updates, suggesting incomplete digital experience
Germantown branch lobby closed, limiting in-person service options at that location
best_for: Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi residents seeking auto financing with low rates and flexible payment terms
First-time credit card users in the credit union's service area wanting introductory 0% APR
Individuals and families looking for a member-focused financial institution with educational support
Small business owners in the Memphis area needing comprehensive business banking and credit union membership benefits
diagnosis: Orion Financial is best for residents of Tennessee, Arkansas, and Mississippi seeking member-owned credit union benefits with competitive auto and mortgage financing, particularly those valuing financial education and community engagement. The primary caveat is geographic restriction—membership requires residency in the service area, and the organization cannot serve customers outside these three states, making it unsuitable for those outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Orion — Credit Union in Memphis, TN | CreditDoc
meta_description: Orion — NCUA-insured credit union in Memphis, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
